one_line_ask: Take the client's supplied composited Continental-stars hero plate (Messi/Ronaldo/Mbappé + trophy already arranged) plus player cut-out and flag-pattern layers, and finish it into a cinematic, high-contrast, flag-colour-accented A2 300-dpi print master plus social-media versions using only Adobe connector tools.
brief:
I am commissioning the FINISHING and COLOUR-GRADE pass on a 2026 Continental Cup hero poster. My photo team has already arranged the licensed shots — Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo and Kylian Mbappé positioned dramatically around the Continental Cup trophy on a night-stadium background — into a single flattened HERO PLATE (5000×7000 px, slightly soft, flat ungraded log-style colour). I am also handing you each player isolated on its own cut-out plate (for selective per-player grading) and a single seamless flag-pattern swatch (a tasteful woven blend of Argentine sky-blue/white, Portuguese red/green, and French blue/white/red) to use as a subtle graphic accent.

Your job, using Adobe tools only, is:
1. Straighten and auto-tone the hero plate, then build a punchy cinematic grade — lift the key/rim contrast so the figures carve out of the dark stadium, warm the floodlight highlights, cool the shadows for depth, and push overall high-contrast "stadium floodlights at night" mood.
2. Weave the national-flag COLOURS in tastefully, not as emblems: use prompt-masking to isolate each player region and nudge the dominant accent colour (Messi → sky-blue energy, Ronaldo → red/green, Mbappé → b
